The transplanted (grafted) trees should be watered both in
the morning and evening everyday in summer; on alternate days in
the cold season; and whenever the soil becomes dry in the rainy
season.

Eugenia, Bayas (or Bent), Vanlra, Naucleaorientalis, Glomerous
hg tree; Arjuna, Bijapura (citron), grape vine; lemon, pomegranate,
Vanjula (spondias), Naktamala (Gray bonduc), Tilaka, jack, Timira
and Amrataka are the sixteen trees that grow in a moist soil.
[The distinction between 3RW, ^1^ and is not
absolute, as the author himself has stated that the trees which are
generally found in moist soil may at times be found growing in other
regions as well.]
Next the author discusses the subject of distance between one
tree and another in a garden.
